title: Villain Methods (Narrative)
entries: Team Death Match - The typical - kill all the enemies before they kill all the good guys style.

Guardian - The fight is focused on a VIP. Ex: Typical Boss (VIP) with supporting minions; Escort, PCs protect a NPC ally (VIP) while they do something or go somewhere; Reverse Escort, NPC allies act to support the PCs, or a PC focused escort mission.

Capture the Flag - Fight over an inanimate McGuffin, like a magic rune or bag of gold. Ex: Once the party defeats the warlord, her underlings will try to grab the body and escape to resurrect her; Try to steal a magic gem that’s guarded by a horde of eternally reanimating skeletons; The party reaches a powerful artifact at the same time as a rival adventuring party; having an objective can force battles to happen in circumstances that aren’t ideal to either side. Can lead to chases and clever uses of non-damaging combat abilities.

King of the Hill - Seek to control a location. This location can start under the control of either faction or start as initially neutral depending on circumstance, and each situation leads to a very different type of encounter. If the location is initially neutral, this functions like a Capture the Flag. Ex: Taking a bridge. One side wants to hold it so that it can be destroyed, another side wants to hold it so that an approaching army can cross. A ‘defensive’ King of the Hill might involve the PCs holding a specific door against enemies that want to burst in and assassinate whoever’s inside. An ‘offensive’ King of the Hill might involve the PCs trying to remain inside a ritual circle to disrupt the summoning of a dark god.

Tower/Wave Defense- Send enemies in waves so that the party doesn't get overwhelmed and to make a combat last more than the traditional three round length. Ex: The party is defending a wall breach against attacking soldiers, or a holy gate against a horde of demons. The enemy can’t attack all at once due to the size of the gap, so they come in waves. Defeat a certain number of enemies, hold out for a certain amount of time, or prevent them from reaching a certain point in order to achieve victory.

Free For All - Survival over body count. The existence of multiple warring factions within a dungeon. If any faction wins decisively, it makes the PCs lives much harder, so it falls the the PCs to ensure that any victory is pyrrhic. EX: An otherwise normal battle is interrupted by a wandering monster looking for an easy meal; A Big Bad’s underling sees the climactic battle with the PCs as the perfect opportunity to betray their boss an eliminate both groups in one fell swoop.

Assassination - The party must eliminate a specific target without being detected. They need to carefully plan their approach, find opportunities to strike discreetly, and consider the consequences of failure, as any commotion could ruin the chance to succeed.

Gauntlet - The party faces a series of increasingly challenging foes without rest. They must endure a relentless onslaught of enemies, each becoming more powerful than the last, testing the limits of their abilities, resourcefulness, and endurance.

Infiltration - The party must sneak past or disguise themselves to avoid direct combat. They need to use stealth, cunning, and subterfuge to navigate through heavily guarded areas or enemy strongholds, all while avoiding detection and maintaining their cover.

Siege - The party is either defending a stronghold from attackers or besieging an enemy stronghold. They must employ strategic positioning, fortifications, and resource management to outlast their opponents in a prolonged conflict of attrition.

Ambush - The enemies surprise the party and have the advantage of positioning. The party must quickly adapt to the unexpected attack, using their wit and combat prowess to turn the tables and emerge victorious.

Trial by Combat - The party faces a series of individual challenges, each representing a different aspect of combat or skill. They must showcase their versatility and proficiency as they overcome these diverse trials to prove their worth or attain a valuable reward.

Escape - The party must navigate through a dangerous area while being pursued by relentless enemies. Their objective is to outrun or outmaneuver their pursuers, making split-second decisions and facing numerous obstacles along the way.

Rescue - The party must reach and free captives or allies while facing numerous obstacles and enemies. They need to balance speed, stealth, and combat to save their friends or valuable hostages from harm.

Last Stand - The party is outnumbered and must hold their ground until reinforcements arrive or until a specific objective is achieved. They must make the most of their resources and find ways to survive against overwhelming odds.

Stealth Assassination - The party must take down enemies one by one without alerting others. They need to use a combination of silent takedowns, distractions, and careful planning to eliminate threats without triggering full-blown combat.

Hunting Party - The party must track and defeat a group of elusive enemies across varied terrain. They must utilize their tracking skills, knowledge of the land, and ability to anticipate their prey's movements to corner and defeat them.

Arena Battle - The party participates in a controlled combat scenario for entertainment or honor. They must showcase their combat prowess and entertain an audience while facing off against various opponents, possibly even allies turned adversaries.

Race Against Time - The party must defeat enemies or reach an objective before a time limit expires. The pressure of the ticking clock forces them to act quickly, for every moment spent in combat or deliberation brings them closer to failure.

Chase - The party is in pursuit of a fleeing enemy or being pursued by relentless adversaries. They must navigate through diverse environments, overcoming obstacles, and deciding when to confront their pursuers or evade them.

Battle of the Bands - The conflict involves a musical or artistic competition where combat skills are used to impress judges or defeat opposing performers. The party must creatively incorporate combat into their performance to outshine or outmaneuver their opponents.

War Games - The conflict is a simulated battle or training exercise to test the party's tactical abilities. They must demonstrate their strategic acumen and adaptability in a controlled setting, facing off against other skilled combatants.

Mystery Combat - The party faces enemies with hidden weaknesses that must be deduced during the fight. They need to carefully observe their foes' actions and patterns to uncover the secret to defeating them.

Gladiatorial Combat - The party is forced to fight in an arena against a variety of opponents for the amusement of an audience. They must rely on their combat skills, showmanship, and ability to entertain the crowd to earn their freedom or rewards.

Siegebreaker - The party must find and exploit weaknesses in a heavily fortified enemy position. They need to analyze the enemy's defenses, discover weak points, and execute a well-coordinated assault to breach their stronghold.

Pursuit - The party must chase down and subdue fleeing enemies before they can escape. They need to pursue their targets through treacherous terrain, overcoming obstacles, and finding ways to close the distance before the adversaries can slip away.

Puzzle Combat - The conflict involves solving complex puzzles or riddles to gain an advantage in battle. The party must use their intellect and problem-solving skills to unlock advantages, disable traps, or weaken their enemies through strategic thinking.

Revenge - The party seeks to avenge a previous defeat or injustice by confronting the responsible enemies. Emotions run high as they fight with the determination to settle old scores and right past wrongs.

Undercover - The party infiltrates enemy ranks, masquerading as foes until the right moment to strike. They must maintain their cover, act convincingly, and seize opportunities to surprise the enemy from within.

Nemesis - The party faces a recurring enemy who continually challenges them throughout their journey. The nemesis is a formidable opponent who grows in power and adaptability with each encounter, becoming a personal rival to the party.

Trial of Champions - The party must defeat a series of powerful champions to prove their worth. Each champion presents unique strengths and weaknesses, testing the party's ability to adapt and find solutions to each challenge.

Cursed Battle - The conflict takes place in an area with a powerful curse that affects combat and tactics. The curse may alter the battlefield, impose limitations on combat abilities, or unleash unforeseen challenges upon the party.

Race to the Finish - The party competes against rivals to reach an objective first while dealing with obstacles and adversaries. They must balance speed and caution, as reaching the goal first may grant them a crucial advantage.

Prison Break - The party must navigate a heavily guarded prison to free an important captive. They need to find ways to bypass security measures, overcome guards, and liberate their ally from captivity.

Battle of Wits - The conflict involves outsmarting and manipulating enemies through diplomacy and deception. Combat becomes a strategic dance of words and bluffing as the party attempts to outwit and subdue their foes without resorting to violence.

Betrayal - In this style, one or more members of the party may betray their allies during the combat, revealing their true allegiance and making the fight even more challenging. The party must not only deal with the external threats posed by the enemies but also uncover the identity of the traitor hiding in their midst. Trust becomes a precious commodity, and the stakes are raised as the party navigates through a web of deception and treachery.

Mind Games - The combat unfolds in a battlefield where enemies employ psychological warfare to confuse, manipulate, or demoralize the party. Illusions, fear-inducing tactics, and exploitation of the party's darkest secrets are among the weapons wielded by the foes. The party must stay vigilant and discern reality from illusion, relying on their mental fortitude and camaraderie to resist the enemy's manipulations and gain the upper hand in the battle of minds.

Infernal Contracts - This combat style centers on enemies who have made infernal deals, granting them otherworldly powers. They are imbued with dark abilities and are formidable adversaries. To defeat these foes, the party may need to find ways to disrupt the infernal contracts, turn the infernal entities against their former allies, or even negotiate with the fiendish patrons themselves. Each decision comes with consequences, and the party must navigate the precarious world of infernal bargains.

Natural Disaster - The battlefield becomes an inhospitable zone as the combat takes place amid a raging natural disaster. Whether it's a volcanic eruption, earthquake, tornado, or other cataclysmic event, the party must not only contend with the onslaught of enemies but also navigate the hazardous environmental effects. They must strategize carefully, using the environment to their advantage while avoiding its destructive forces.

Dimensional Rift - In this style, the combat occurs near a rift between dimensions, causing strange and unpredictable phenomena to manifest on the battlefield. The party must adapt quickly to the shifting environment, facing foes from other planes of existence that emerge from the unstable rift. As reality bends and twists, the party's ability to strategize and make split-second decisions becomes crucial in this multi-dimensional battle.

Hidden Influence - The combat involves enemies who are secretly being controlled or influenced by a powerful entity. The party must identify the puppeteer pulling the strings and find a way to sever the control over their adversaries. This hidden influence adds an extra layer of complexity to the battle, as the true nature of the enemy's allegiance and motivations remains concealed until the party uncovers the truth.

Sacrifice - In this style, the party must make difficult decisions during combat, choosing between protecting innocent bystanders, valuable artifacts, or fellow adventurers. These choices have consequences that can impact the outcome of the battle and future events. The party may be torn between their desire to uphold their principles and their need to achieve their goals, leading to emotionally charged moments and moral dilemmas.

Desperation - The enemies in this combat style are cornered and fighting for their lives, making them more unpredictable and dangerous. They might employ reckless tactics, unleash devastating abilities, or fight with newfound ferocity in a last-ditch effort to survive. The party must be cautious and adapt their strategies on the fly as desperate foes can prove to be unexpectedly formidable opponents.

War of Words - In this combat style, battles revolve around verbal dueling and debate. The enemies may be persuaded, intimidated, or convinced to abandon their aggression or even surrender. Charisma, persuasion, and intimidation skills play a crucial role in determining the outcome. The party must use their wit, eloquence, and clever arguments to sway the hearts and minds of their adversaries, turning enemies into allies or de-escalating conflicts through the power of words.

Eclipse - The combat takes place during a rare celestial event, such as a solar or lunar eclipse. The alignment of celestial bodies grants temporary boons or vulnerabilities to the party and enemies alike. The party may harness the celestial energies to gain unique abilities, while the foes might exploit the same forces against the party. The eclipse adds an element of unpredictability to the battlefield, forcing the party to adapt their strategies and make the most of the celestial phenomena.

Trial of Virtues - In this combat style, the party faces a test of their virtues and principles. They must adhere to strict ethical guidelines while battling foes who may use trickery and deception to tempt them to forsake their values. The party's commitment to their virtues may be rewarded with divine intervention or other blessings, while compromising their beliefs could lead to unforeseen consequences. The choices the party makes in this trial will shape their characters and moral compass.

Memory Alteration - The party faces enemies with the ability to manipulate memories, causing confusion and uncertainty about their past actions and allegiances. The foes may employ illusions, false memories, or mind-altering magic to challenge the party's perceptions. The party must navigate the fog of altered memories, drawing on their intuition and bond with each other to distinguish reality from illusion and identify true allies and adversaries.

Emotional Tug-of-War - This combat style involves enemies who can manipulate the emotions of the party and use their feelings against them. The foes might induce fear, anger, or despair in the party members, attempting to exploit their emotional vulnerabilities. To emerge victorious, the party must maintain control over their emotions, displaying mental resilience and emotional intelligence to prevent being emotionally manipulated.

Time Dilation - The combat occurs within a realm where time flows differently. The party may experience time speeding up, slowing down, or even rewinding, resulting in unpredictable temporal effects on the battlefield. The party must adapt quickly to the ever-changing circumstances, seizing opportunities presented by temporal anomalies and managing the challenges they pose.

Curse of Transformation - In this combat style, the party faces enemies cursed to transform into powerful creatures during the battle. The curse may grant them enhanced abilities, but it also leaves them vulnerable to specific attacks or conditions. The party must identify the curse's source or weakness and strategize to exploit it. Timing and coordination become crucial as the party must strike when the enemies are at their weakest form.

Stealing the Essence - The enemies in this combat style seek to drain the life force or vital essence of the party members. They may have dark rituals or draining abilities that threaten the party's very existence. The party must protect themselves from these malevolent forces and devise countermeasures to prevent their energy from being siphoned away. The battle becomes a race against time, with the party aiming to defeat the enemies before succumbing to their draining powers.

Conscious Environment - The battlefield itself is alive or sentient, actively hindering or assisting the combatants based on their actions. The environment may react to certain spells, attacks, or tactics used by the party or enemies. For example, certain areas of the battlefield may become hazardous when attacked, while others may provide temporary cover or boons to those who interact with them creatively. The party must find ways to cooperate with or manipulate the environment to their advantage while thwarting the enemies' attempts to do the same.

Broken Mirror - The combat takes place in a parallel reality or mirrored dimension, where reflections come to life and challenge the party. They must confront their own doppelgängers, each mirroring the party's abilities and tactics, making the fight an intense battle of wits and skills. The party must outsmart and outmaneuver their mirror images, potentially using their knowledge of themselves against their doppelgängers.

One True Love - In this combat style, the enemy holds someone dear to the heart of a party member hostage, using them as leverage to force the party into submission. The party is faced with a moral dilemma, balancing the urgency of saving their loved one with fulfilling their mission and protecting the greater good. They must carefully strategize and navigate the battlefield to both neutralize the enemies and ensure the safety of their beloved ally.

Battle of the Elements - This combat style revolves around harnessing the power of the elements to gain an advantage. The party and enemies may wield elemental magic or weapons, leading to spectacular displays of elemental forces clashing in epic battles. The environment may change dynamically as the elements interact, creating opportunities for tactical maneuvers and devastating attacks. The party must master the elements and use them strategically to gain the upper hand in the elemental clash.

Divine Intervention - During the combat, a deity or divine entity may intervene, either aiding or opposing the party's efforts. The party may receive divine blessings, visions, or guidance to strengthen them for the battle ahead. However, the divine being's motives and intentions may be mysterious and inscrutable, requiring the party to stay true to their beliefs and convictions while navigating the unpredictable actions of godlike beings.

Perilous Terrain - The battlefield is riddled with dangerous obstacles, traps, or environmental hazards that the party must navigate while facing enemies. The party needs to utilize mobility, spatial awareness, and ingenuity to gain the upper hand. They may need to engage in aerial combat, swing across chasms, or cleverly utilize the terrain to outmaneuver their foes and claim victory.
